fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('Role & Profile', {'fields': ('role', 'bio', 'avatar_url')}))

list_filter = ('role', 'is_active', 'is_staff')
property media
ordering = ('-date_joined',)

ready()[source]

Override this method in subclasses to run code when Django starts.

accounts.decorators module

accounts.forms module

Forms used by the accounts app.

This module provides Django form classes for registration, authentication, and profile editing. It customizes widgets and field ordering for a consistent Bootstrap-friendly UI.

class accounts.forms.LoginForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: AuthenticationForm

Authentication form with custom widget styling.

base_fields = {'password': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'username': <django.contrib.auth.forms.UsernameField object>}
declared_fields = {'password': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'username': <django.contrib.auth.forms.UsernameField object>}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

Edit form for a user’s public profile fields.

class accounts.forms.RegisterForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: UserCreationForm

Form used to register a new user.

Extends Django’s UserCreationForm to collect first name, last name, email and role in addition to username and password fields.

class accounts.management_forms.UserEditForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelForm

Editors can update any user’s profile and role. Reader-specific M2M fields are shown only when role=reader. Journalist content fields are read-only reverse relations (shown in template).

Check if the email provided is in use, Ensures emails are unique.

declared_fields = {'role': <django.forms.fields.ChoiceField object>, 'subscribed_journalists': <django.forms.models.ModelMultipleChoiceField object>, 'subscribed_publishers': <django.forms.models.ModelMultipleChoiceField object>}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

save(commit=True)[source]

Save new user.

accounts.management_views module

Management views for user administration.

These views are protected by the editor_required decorator and are intended for editors to list, inspect, create, edit and deactivate user accounts. Small docstrings and inline comments clarify behavior for maintainers and template authors.

accounts.management_views.user_create_view(request)[source]

Create a new user from the management interface.

On success redirects to the newly created user’s detail page and flashes a message confirming account creation.

accounts.management_views.user_deactivate_view(request, pk)[source]

Toggle the is_active flag for a user.

Editors cannot deactivate their own account. The endpoint expects a POST request to perform the state toggle and provides feedback via Django messages.

accounts.management_views.user_detail_view(request, pk)[source]

Show detailed information about a single user.

Returns the most recent articles and newsletters authored by the target user, basic approval stats, and role-specific context used by the admin templates.

accounts.management_views.user_edit_view(request, pk)[source]

Edit an existing user’s account.

If the form is valid the changes are saved and the admin is redirected to the user’s detail page with a success message.

accounts.management_views.user_list_view(request)[source]

List users for administrative purposes.

Supports optional filtering by search query, role and active status. Annotates each user with counts of related Article and Newsletter objects for convenient display in the template.

accounts.models module

Account models for the project.

This module defines CustomUser, an extension of Django’s AbstractUser that adds role-based behavior (reader, journalist, editor), subscription relations for readers, and convenience properties for role checks and related content access.

The model also ensures the user is assigned to an appropriate Django Group on save and keeps reader-only subscription fields empty for non-readers.

class accounts.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: AbstractUser

Custom user model with role, subscriptions and helpers.

Fields added: - role: determines permissions and group membership. - bio, avatar_url: optional profile fields. - subscribed_publishers, subscribed_journalists: M2M fields used by readers to follow publishers and journalists.

The class exposes properties like is_reader, can_create_content, and related managers (journalist_articles, journalist_newsletters) to simplify view and template logic.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

ROLE_CHOICES = [('reader', 'Reader'), ('journalist', 'Journalist'), ('editor', 'Editor')]
USERNAME_FIELD = 'username'
articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

auth_token

Accessor to the related object on the reverse side of a one-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Restaurant(Model):
    place = OneToOneField(Place, related_name='restaurant')

Place.restaurant is a ReverseOneToOneDescriptor instance.

avatar_url

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

bio

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

property can_approve
property can_create_content
date_joined

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

editing_publishers

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

email

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

first_name

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_previous_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=False, **kwargs)
get_role_display(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.CharField: role>)
get_role_display_badge()[source]
groups

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

id

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

is_active

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

property is_editor
property is_journalist
property is_reader
is_staff

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

is_superuser

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

property journalist_articles

Returns articles authored by this journalist, None for non-journalists.

property journalist_independent_articles

Articles published independently (no publisher). None for non-journalists.

property journalist_independent_newsletters

Newsletters published independently. None for non-journalists.

property journalist_newsletters

Returns newsletters authored by this journalist, None for non-journalists.

journalist_subscribers

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

property reader_subscribed_journalists

Returns the subscribed_journalists queryset for readers, None for other roles.

property reader_subscribed_publishers

Returns the subscribed_publishers queryset for readers, None for other roles.

role

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

save(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Persist the user and enforce role-related side effects.

After saving the model fields, ensure the user’s Django Group membership reflects their role and clear any reader-only subscription relations when the role is not reader.

subscribed_journalists

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

accounts.signals module

accounts.signals.create_auth_token(sender, instance=None, created=False, **kwargs)[source]

Automatically generates a DRF Token whenever a new user is saved.

accounts.signals.create_groups(sender, **kwargs)[source]

Triggers the group setup after migrations finish.

accounts.urls module

accounts.views module

Views for user accounts: registration, authentication, profiles.

This module contains simple Django view functions used by the accounts app. Each view handles request/response logic and uses Django forms and messages for user feedback.

accounts.views.journalist_list_view(request)[source]

List all users that have the journalist role.

The view orders journalists by last name and renders a simple list template.

Parameters:

request – Django HttpRequest

Returns:

rendered list of journalists.

Return type:

HttpResponse

accounts.views.journalist_public_profile(request, pk)[source]

Show a public profile for a journalist.

  • If the requested profile belongs to the currently authenticated journalist, redirect them to their editable profile page.

  • Otherwise, show the journalist’s public information and a list of their approved articles (limited to 10 most recent).

Parameters:

  • request – Django HttpRequest

  • pk – int - primary key of the CustomUser with role ‘journalist’

Returns:

rendered public profile page.

Return type:

HttpResponse

accounts.views.login_view(request)[source]

Authenticate and log a user in.

  • Redirect authenticated users to the dashboard.

  • On POST, validate LoginForm, log the user in and redirect to next parameter if provided, otherwise to the dashboard.

Parameters:

request – Django HttpRequest

Returns:

render of login page or redirect on success.

Return type:

HttpResponse

accounts.views.logout_view(request)[source]

Log the current user out and redirect to the login page.

Parameters:

request – Django HttpRequest

Returns:

redirect to the login page.

Return type:

HttpResponseRedirect

accounts.views.profile_view(request)[source]

Display and update the authenticated user’s profile.

Uses ProfileForm bound to request.user. On successful POST the profile is saved and the user is redirected back to the profile page.

Parameters:

request – Django HttpRequest

Returns:

render of profile page.

Return type:

HttpResponse

accounts.views.register_view(request)[source]

Handle user registration.

  • Redirect authenticated users to the dashboard.

  • On POST, validate RegisterForm, create the user, log them in, show a success message and redirect to the dashboard.

Parameters:

request – Django HttpRequest

Returns:

render of registration page or redirect on success.

Return type:

HttpResponse

accounts.views.toggle_subscription(request, target_type, target_id)[source]

Toggle subscription for the authenticated reader.

Readers can subscribe/unsubscribe to journalists or publishers.

Parameters:

  • request – Django HttpRequest

  • target_type – str - either ‘journalist’ or ‘publisher’

  • target_id – int - primary key of the target object

Returns:

redirect back to the referring page or dashboard if no referrer is present.

Return type:

HttpResponseRedirect
